Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Nanotwinned precipitates induced ultra-strong AlCoCrFeNi_{2.1} eutectic high-entropy alloy through additive manufacturing

Composition_Info.Note: Powder nominal formula is AlCoCrFeNi2.1 (at%); measured as-built composition totals ~79.6 at%, balance attributed to oxygen in powder. B2 phase measured composition: Al15.9Co16.9Cr15.7Fe16.8Ni34.7 (at%).

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized AlCoCrFeNi2.1 powder (particle size 15-53 µm, avg 31.5 µm) fabricated by LangFeng Material Technology Co., Ltd.; PBF-LB performed on EOS M100 (200 W fiber laser, 40 µm spot diameter) with optimized parameters: laser power 100 W, scan speed 1000 mm/s, hatch spacing 0.05 mm, layer thickness 0.02 mm; no preheating; high-purity argon atmosphere (<1000 ppm oxygen); scan direction rotated 67° per layer; final printed rectangular plates 40 mm × 8 mm × 4 mm building height; relative density >99%.
Process_Text.simplified: PBF-LB (EOS M100, 100 W, 1000 mm/s, 0.05 mm hatch, 0.02 mm layer, 40 µm spot, no preheat, Ar atmosphere, 67° inter-layer rotation); relative density >99%.

Process_Info.Equipment: EOS M100 Laser-sintering system (200 W fiber laser)

Microstructure_Text.original: Dual-phase hierarchical microstructure with nano-lamellar and cellular eutectic colonies. Lamellar colonies: alternating FCC (λFCC = 87 nm) and ordered B2 (λB2 = 160 nm) nanolayers with K-S orientation relationship. Cellular colonies: BCC subgrains (~800 nm) with ordered-B2 structure encapsulated by disordered FCC interfacial nanolayer (~14 nm), N-W orientation relationship. No precipitates in as-built condition. High dislocation density. B2 volume fraction 83.6%, FCC 16.4%. Biconinuous Ni-Al-rich and Co-Cr-Fe-rich nanostructures within B2 colonies indicating spinodal decomposition.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Dual-phase B2-dominant (83.6 vol%) microstructure with nano-lamellar and cellular eutectic colonies; K-S and N-W orientation relationships; high dislocation density; no precipitates; spinodal decomposition in B2 colonies.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.XRD: Bruker D8 Advance, Cu-Kα, 40 kV/40 mA, 2θ 20°-100°
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM: FEI Scios with HKL EBSD system; mechanically ground and electrolytically polished
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: JEOL JEM-2100F at 200 kV; Cs-corrected FEI Themis Z at 300 kV for HAADF-STEM and HRTEM
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.APT: CAMECA LEAP 5000XR at 70 K, voltage mode, 200 kHz, 20% pulse fraction
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.EDS: STEM-EDS for compositional analysis of all phases

Composition_Info.Note: Powder nominal formula is AlCoCrFeNi2.1 (at%); measured as-built composition totals ~79.6 at%, balance attributed to oxygen in powder. Post-annealing at 600°C for 8h water quench does not change overall composition. B2 phase measured composition: Al15.9Co16.9Cr15.7Fe16.8Ni34.7 (at%). NT FCC precipitates composition: Co24.4Cr24.4Fe24.4Ni24.4Al2.4 (at%).

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized AlCoCrFeNi2.1 powder (particle size 15-53 µm, avg 31.5 µm) fabricated by LangFeng Material Technology Co., Ltd.; PBF-LB performed on EOS M100 (200 W fiber laser, 40 µm spot diameter) with optimized parameters: laser power 100 W, scan speed 1000 mm/s, hatch spacing 0.05 mm, layer thickness 0.02 mm; no preheating; high-purity argon atmosphere (<1000 ppm oxygen); scan direction rotated 67° per layer; final printed rectangular plates 40 mm × 8 mm × 4 mm building height; relative density >99%. Post-heat treatment: annealing at 600°C for 8 h in muffle furnace followed by water quenching.
Process_Text.simplified: PBF-LB (EOS M100, 100 W, 1000 mm/s, 0.05 mm hatch, 0.02 mm layer, 40 µm spot, no preheat, Ar atmosphere, 67° rotation) followed by annealing at 600°C for 8 h, water quench.

Process_Info.Equipment: EOS M100 Laser-sintering system (200 W fiber laser); Muffle furnace (post-heat treatment)

Microstructure_Text.original: Multiple-order hierarchical multiphase architecture after annealing at 600°C for 8 h. Retains mixture of nano-lamellar and cellular eutectic colonies with slight coarsening: λFCC from 87→96 nm, λB2 from 160→176 nm, cellular diameter ~549→653 nm. High-density needle-like nanoprecipitates (~180 nm length, ~20 nm diameter, aspect ratio ~8.75) evenly distributed in B2 phases. Two distinct precipitate types: Type I (FCC, width >20 nm) with ultrafine nanotwins (twin thickness 0.4-16 nm, avg 2.4 nm), 76% of needle-like precipitates; Type II (HCP, width <10 nm) with high-density stacking faults (SF spacing 0.4-2.9 nm, avg 1.1 nm), ~24% of needle-like precipitates. 76% of needle-like nanoprecipitates are FCC-type. L12 precipitates identified in FCC phase (superlattice SAED spots). NT FCC precipitates are semi-coherent with B2 matrix (K-S relationship). HCP precipitates follow Burgers orientation relationship with B2 matrix. Volume fractions: 20% in lamellar colonies, 45% in cellular colonies. APT: Co, Cr, Fe partitioned into NT FCC precipitates; Ni and Al depleted. NT precipitate composition: CoCrFeNi ~24.4 at.% each, Al 2.4 at.% (CoCrFeNiAl0.1 HEA).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: B2-dominant microstructure (83.6 vol%) with high-density NT FCC needle-like precipitates (76%, avg twin thickness 2.4 nm) and HCP precipitates with stacking faults (24%); L12 in FCC phase; semi-coherent precipitate-matrix interfaces with K-S and Burgers orientation relationships; NT precipitates correspond to CoCrFeNiAl0.1 HEA.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.EDS: STEM-EDS for compositional analysis of all phases; APT for 3D compositional mapping
